:PROPERTIES:
:GDOCS_DOCUMENT_ID: kitchen-sink-test
:END:
#+TITLE: Kitchen sink
#+AUTHOR: Test Author
#+DATE: <2026-03-22 Sun>
#+EMAIL: test@example.com
#+LANGUAGE: en
#+STARTUP: overview
#+TODO: TODO NEXT | DONE CANCELLED
#+FILETAGS: :test:kitchen-sink:
#+MACRO: version 1.0.0
#+LINK: wp https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%s

* Paragraph styles

** Normal paragraphs

A simple paragraph with no formatting.

A second paragraph separated by a blank line.  This one has two sentences.

** Block quotes

#+BEGIN_QUOTE
To be, or not to be, that is the question.
#+END_QUOTE

** Center blocks

#+BEGIN_CENTER
This text is centered.
#+END_CENTER

** Verse blocks

#+BEGIN_VERSE
Great clouds overhead
Tiny black birds rise and fall \\
Snow covers Emacs
#+END_VERSE

** Example blocks

#+BEGIN_EXAMPLE
This is an example block.
  It preserves indentation.
    And whitespace.
#+END_EXAMPLE

** Source code blocks

#+BEGIN_SRC emacs-lisp
(defun hello ()
  (message "Hello, world!"))
#+END_SRC

#+BEGIN_SRC python :results output
def fibonacci(n):
    a, b = 0, 1
    for _ in range(n):
        a, b = b, a + b
    return a

print(fibonacci(10))
#+END_SRC

#+BEGIN_SRC shell
echo "Hello from shell"
#+END_SRC

** Export blocks

#+BEGIN_EXPORT html
<div class="custom">Raw HTML content</div>
#+END_EXPORT

** Special blocks

#+BEGIN_WARNING
This is a warning block.
#+END_WARNING

#+BEGIN_NOTE
This is a note block.
#+END_NOTE

** Comment blocks

#+BEGIN_COMMENT
This comment block should not appear in output.
#+END_COMMENT

** Fixed-width areas

: This is fixed-width text.
: It uses a colon prefix.

* Text styles

This paragraph has *bold text*, /italic text/, _underlined text_,
+struck-through text+, ~inline code~, and =verbatim text=.

** Nested formatting

This has */bold italic/*, *_bold underline_*, /_italic underline_/,
*/+bold italic strikethrough+/*, and *~bold code~*.

** Consecutive formatting

*Bold* followed by /italic/ followed by _underline_ followed by +strikethrough+.

** Formatting at boundaries

*Bold at start* of paragraph.

Paragraph ending in *bold*.

A *single-word* formatted.

* Links

** Web links

Visit [[https://orgmode.org][Org mode website]] for more information.

A plain link: https://www.example.com

An angle link: <https://www.example.com/path>

** Internal links

See section [[*Paragraph styles]] for more.

A dedicated target: <<my-target>>

Jump to [[my-target]].

** Link with formatting

A link with *bold*: [[https://example.com][*Bold link text*]].

** Various link types

- File link: [[file:other-file.org][Another file]]
- File link with heading: [[file:other-file.org::*Introduction][Intro section]]
- ID link: [[id:12345678-abcd-1234-abcd-123456789abc][An ID link]]
- Custom ID link: [[#my-custom-id][Custom ID]]
- Mailto link: [[mailto:user@example.com][Email us]]
- Shell link: [[shell:echo "hello"]]
- Elisp link: [[elisp:(message "hi")]]
- Code ref link: [[(my-label)]]

** Radio targets

This paragraph defines <<<important concept>>> as a radio target.

Later, the text important concept automatically becomes a link.

* Lists

** Unordered lists

- First item
- Second item
- Third item

** Nested unordered lists

- Level 0 item A
  - Level 1 item A.1
    - Level 2 item A.1.1
    - Level 2 item A.1.2
  - Level 1 item A.2
- Level 0 item B

** Ordered lists

1. First item
2. Second item
3. Third item

** Nested ordered lists

1. Level 0 item
   1. Level 1 item
      1. Level 2 item
      2. Level 2 item
   2. Level 1 item
2. Level 0 item

** Checkbox lists

- [ ] Unchecked item
- [X] Checked item
- [-] Partially checked item

** Descriptive lists

- Emacs :: An extensible text editor.
- Org mode :: A major mode for note-taking and project planning.
- gdocs :: An Emacs package for syncing with Google Docs.

** Mixed nested lists

- Bullet parent
  1. Numbered child 1
  2. Numbered child 2
     - Bullet grandchild
       1. Numbered great-grandchild

** List with formatted content

- *Bold item*
- /Italic item/ with [[https://example.com][a link]]
- Item with ~code~ and =verbatim=

** Consecutive numbered list groups

First group:

1. Alpha
2. Beta
3. Gamma

Second group:

1. One
2. Two
3. Three

* Tables

** Basic table

| Name  | Age | City       |
|-------+-----+------------|
| Alice |  30 | New York   |
| Bob   |  25 | London     |
| Carol |  35 | Tokyo      |

** Table with formatting

| *Header 1* | *Header 2* | *Header 3* |
|-------------+------------+------------|
| /italic/    | ~code~     | _under_    |
| *bold*      | +struck+   | normal     |

** Table with alignment

|   <l>   |  <c>  |  <r>  |
| Left    | Center | Right |
|---------+--------+-------|
| aligned | text   | here  |

** Table with formulas

| Item   | Price | Quantity | Total |
|--------+-------+----------+-------|
| Widget |  9.99 |        3 | 29.97 |
| Gadget | 24.99 |        1 | 24.99 |
|--------+-------+----------+-------|
| Total  |       |          | 54.96 |
#+TBLFM: $4=$2*$3;%.2f::@>$4=vsum(@2..@-1);%.2f

* Headings

** Level 2 heading

*** Level 3 heading

**** Level 4 heading

***** Level 5 heading

****** Level 6 heading

******* Level 7 heading (clamped to 6)

* Org-specific features

** TODO keywords

*** TODO A task that needs doing

*** NEXT A task in progress

*** DONE A completed task
CLOSED: [2026-03-22 Sun 10:00]

*** CANCELLED A cancelled task

** Priority

*** [#A] High priority task

*** [#B] Medium priority task

*** [#C] Low priority task

** Tags
*** Heading with tags                                              :work:urgent:

*** Heading with multiple tags                              :project:review:code:

** Planning

*** TODO Scheduled task
SCHEDULED: <2026-04-01 Wed>

*** TODO Deadline task
DEADLINE: <2026-04-15 Wed>

*** TODO Both scheduled and deadline
SCHEDULED: <2026-04-01 Wed> DEADLINE: <2026-04-15 Wed>

** Properties
*** Heading with properties
:PROPERTIES:
:CUSTOM_ID: my-custom-id
:CREATED: [2026-03-22 Sun]
:CATEGORY: testing
:END:

This heading has a property drawer.

** Statistics cookies

*** Project [2/5]
- [X] Task 1
- [X] Task 2
- [ ] Task 3
- [ ] Task 4
- [ ] Task 5

*** Progress [40%]
- [X] Step 1
- [X] Step 2
- [ ] Step 3
- [ ] Step 4
- [ ] Step 5

** Clocking

*** Clocked task
:LOGBOOK:
CLOCK: [2026-03-22 Sun 09:00]--[2026-03-22 Sun 10:30] =>  1:30
CLOCK: [2026-03-21 Sat 14:00]--[2026-03-21 Sat 15:15] =>  1:15
:END:

* Inline elements

** Timestamps

Active timestamp: <2026-03-22 Sun>

Active with time: <2026-03-22 Sun 14:30>

Inactive timestamp: [2026-03-22 Sun]

Inactive with time: [2026-03-22 Sun 14:30]

Date range: <2026-03-22 Sun>--<2026-03-29 Sun>

Time range: <2026-03-22 Sun 09:00-17:00>

Repeating: <2026-03-22 Sun +1w>

Repeating catch-up: <2026-03-22 Sun ++1d>

Repeating restart: <2026-03-22 Sun .+1m>

With warning: <2026-04-15 Wed -3d>

** Entities

Greek letters: \alpha, \beta, \gamma, \delta, \pi, \Omega

Arrows: \to, \rightarrow, \Rightarrow, \leftarrow

Symbols: \infty, \pm, \times, \div, \le, \ge, \ne

Typography: \mdash, \ndash, \hellip, \trade, \copy, \reg

Special: \nbsp, \dag, \ddag, \amp

** Subscript and superscript

Water is H_2O.

Energy equals mc^2.

Subscript with braces: a_{i,j}.

Superscript with braces: e^{i\pi}.

** LaTeX fragments

Inline math: $E = mc^2$

Inline with parens: \(x^2 + y^2 = z^2\)

Display math: \[\sum_{i=1}^{n} i = \frac{n(n+1)}{2}\]

** LaTeX environments

\begin{equation}
\int_0^\infty e^{-x}\,dx = 1
\end{equation}

\begin{align}
a &= b + c \\
d &= e + f
\end{align}

** Footnotes

This text has a footnote reference[fn:1].

An inline footnote[fn:2:This is defined inline.].

An anonymous footnote[fn::This has no label.].

[fn:1] This is the first footnote definition.

** Inline source code and babel

The result of src_emacs-lisp{(+ 1 2)} is 3.

Call a named block: call_my-function(x=1)

** Macros

The version is {{{version}}}.

The author is {{{author}}}.

** Export snippets

This is an HTML export snippet: @@html:<mark>highlighted</mark>@@.

This is a LaTeX snippet: @@latex:\textsc{Small Caps}@@.

** Line breaks

First line \\
Second line \\
Third line

** Citations

A citation: [cite:@knuth84].

With style: [cite/t:@knuth84].

Multiple references: [cite:@knuth84;@lamport94].

* Horizontal rules

Content above the rule.

-----

Content below the rule.

-----

Content between two rules.

-----

* Comments

# This is a comment line.
# It should not appear in output.

* Affiliated keywords

#+CAPTION: A captioned table
#+NAME: my-named-table
| A | B |
|---+---|
| 1 | 2 |

#+CAPTION[Short]: A long caption for the next element
#+ATTR_HTML: :width 80%
#+BEGIN_EXAMPLE
Attributed example block
#+END_EXAMPLE

* Drawers

** Heading with drawer
:MYDATA:
Some custom drawer content.
Multiple lines are allowed.
:END:

Paragraph after the drawer.

* Dynamic blocks

#+BEGIN: clocktable :maxlevel 2 :scope file
#+END:

* Diary sexps

%%(org-anniversary 2000 1 15) Birthday: John Doe (%d years old)

* Inline tasks

This requires org-inlinetask to be loaded.
